hypothesis: in-memory per-bot rate limit prevents auth DoS without adding tower-governor dep
reasoning: Global+per-bot caps on pending challenges prevent memory exhaustion without new dependency
learnings: DashMap iteration is O(n) but acceptable for 10K items
Constant-time secret comparison needs explicit implementation

hypothesis: shared-secret auth on validator server prevents unauthorized signing
reasoning: Axum middleware with constant-time bearer comparison; opt-in via VALIDATOR_SHARED_SECRET env
learnings: Axum middleware layers run in reverse order of registration
Constant-time comparison must XOR all bytes before returning

hypothesis: Secret<T> wrapper with redacted Debug prevents accidental key leakage
reasoning: In-tree alternative to secrecy crate avoids new dependency while forcing explicit expose() calls
learnings: Every caller of .expose() is a potential audit point — grep-friendly

hypothesis: sync disk persist before CLOB submission prevents replay on crash
reasoning: CLOB orders are irreversible off-chain; best-effort persistence is insufficient
learnings: Defense-in-depth matters most for off-chain irreversible operations
Error paths must return 500 BEFORE submitting to external systems

hypothesis: raising virtual offset from 1 to 1e6 strengthens inflation attack resistance without breaking flows
reasoning: OZ defaults to offset=1 but production recommendation is 1e3-1e6; larger values make inflation attacks economically infeasible
learnings: Virtual offset changes introduce rounding proportional to offset size
Existing tests with 1-wei tolerance need to widen to match new offset
1e6 offset costs ~0.0000001% precision on standard 18-decimal assets

hypothesis: adding getBrokenVaults() view surfaces silently-excluded vaults to admins
reasoning: totalNAV() try/catches broken vaults silently; admins need a way to detect this without off-chain vault-by-vault testing
learnings: View functions can't emit events; use dedicated query methods instead
Separate visibility endpoint from business logic to avoid gas cost on happy path

hypothesis: error message redaction prevents internal detail leakage to HTTP clients
reasoning: alloy error messages may echo key format details; log server-side with tracing, return generic message to clients
learnings: Always separate server-side logging (detailed) from client-side error responses (generic)
tracing::error! is the right pattern for PII-adjacent errors
